title: He Ran His First Marathon in Under 2 Hours — and Still Got 2nd Place!
summary: Imagine running your very first marathon — 26.2 miles! — and finishing in less than two hours. That's exactly what Yomif Kejelcha did at the London Marathon on Sunday. His time was 1:59:41. That's faster than the 2-hour goal that runners have dreamed about for years. But here's the twist: he didn't win! He came in second place. Sabastian Sawe of Kenya finished first with an even faster time. Still, Kejelcha was not upset. "I'm very, very happy because I broke two hours," he said. Before the race, Kejelcha didn't think he could do it. He thought he might finish in 2 hours and 2 or 3 minutes. But during the race, he and Sawe pushed each other. They ran side by side for most of it. With one mile left, Sawe pulled ahead. Kejelcha looked at his watch near the finish line and saw he was under two hours. He couldn't believe it. "This is so crazy," he said. Kejelcha is from Ethiopia and has won other big races, but this was his first marathon. He said he and Sawe are friends and have a friendly competition. Another runner, Jacob Kiplimo from Uganda, also beat the old world record but came in third. For Kejelcha, just breaking the 2-hour mark was a dream come true.
why_it_matters: Kejelcha's story shows that doing your personal best can feel like a victory, even if you don't come in first place.

keywords.explanation: A very long running race that is exactly 26.2 miles (42.2 kilometers) long.
The fastest time anyone has ever achieved in an official race.
Finished in less than two hours, which is a very fast time for a marathon.
When two people try to beat each other but still respect and like each other.
An unexpected and surprising change in a story.
Something someone really wanted to happen that finally happened.

background_read: The London Marathon is a famous race that started in 1981. It takes place every year on the streets of London, England.
For a long time, runners thought a sub-2-hour marathon was impossible, but a special record was set in 2019 by Eliud Kipchoge.
Countries like Ethiopia and Kenya are known for training some of the world's best long-distance runners.
Article_Structure: WHO: Yomif Kejelcha, a runner from Ethiopia, and Sabastian Sawe from Kenya
WHAT: Kejelcha ran his first marathon in under 2 hours but finished second
WHERE: The London Marathon in London, England
WHY: Because he broke the 2-hour mark, a longtime goal for runners, he was very happy anyway

perspectives.description: He was overjoyed to finish in under two hours in his first marathon. He didn't mind coming in second because breaking the barrier was his dream.
Fans are amazed that three runners beat the old world record in the same race. They see it as a sign that marathon running is getting faster and more exciting.
